title: Punjene paprike (stuffed peppers)
description: Sweet peppers filled with a savoury meat-and-rice mix, slow-baked in a light tomato base — a Sunday table favourite across the Balkans.

ingredients.item: bell peppers, tops cut and seeded
mixed minced meat (beef and pork, or as Jove uses)
Salt, black pepper, sweet paprika, dried summer savoury (especially if you have it)

steps: Sauté onion in oil until soft, add garlic for the last minute. Mix with mince, raw rice, paprika, salt, and pepper (and savoury if using). Lightly pack into hollowed peppers; replace caps if you kept them.
Place peppers snugly in a deep baking dish. Pour passata around (not over the filling so it doesn’t wash out) and add stock to come halfway up the peppers.
Cover and bake in a moderate oven until peppers collapse and the rice is tender, basting once or twice. Uncover for the last stretch for a little colour on top.
Rest 10 minutes before serving — the filling sets and the flavour mellows. Spoon pan juices over each plate.
notes: This is a starter recipe: swap herbs and spice levels to match how Jove seasons at home. You can add finely diced carrot to the filling for sweetness.
